## Structure

TCP S→C 0x8386 — `CharOpAck` (character-operation
acknowledgement). Single response opcode for every
`0x84 0x82` (GetCharList) sub-operation. Verified 2026-05-10
against `CharOpAck.java` source matching all 4 retail samples.

```
Success / preview-ack form (7 bytes):
  [0..1]  83 86             TCP opcode
  [2]     01                CONSTANT (success path)
  [3..5]  00 00 00          CONSTANT
  [6]     status u8          variant byte (see enum below)

Error form (variable):
  [0..1]  83 86             TCP opcode
  [2]     06                CONSTANT (error path)
  [3]     00                CONSTANT
  [4..5]  msg_len LE16      ASCII message length
  [6..]   ASCII message     no null terminator
```

Status byte enum (success path):

| Status | Meaning                | Triggered by         |
|--------|------------------------|----------------------|
| 0x00   | create-commit success  | op=7 CREATE_CHAR commit |
| 0x05   | delete success         | op=3 DELETE_CHAR     |
| 0x3d   | name-check / preview ok| op=5 CHECK_NAME      |

Verified samples:
```
83 86 01 00 00 00 00     create commit success
83 86 01 00 00 00 05     delete success
83 86 01 00 00 00 3d     name-check OK
83 86 06 00 29 00 [ASCII "User already online, cannot delete char." 00 01]
                  ^len=41
   delete error — char is currently logged in
```

## Variants

Two distinct forms:
- **7-byte success/preview** (3/4 samples): pure constant body
  with one status byte at [6].
- **Variable error** (1/4 samples): `06 00 [len LE16] [ASCII message]`
  with no null terminator.

The error message is human-readable English text that the
client displays directly in the char-management UI.

## Observed contexts

Concentrated in the captures that exercise char operations:
- `RETAIL_CREATION_LEVELING_LONG` × 2 (char creation flow:
  preview + commit)
- `RETAIL_CHARDEL_SUBWAY` × 2 (char deletion: success +
  error)

The 13 captures that DON'T see this are sessions that didn't
create or delete a character.

## Open questions

- Are there other status bytes beyond 0x00, 0x05, 0x3d? The 4
  samples we have cover only 3 distinct values.
- Does the error form use the SAME 0x06 prefix for all error
  cases, or are there other error-prefixes?

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_tcp.CharOpAck` — implements
both forms via factory methods (no direct constructor). Status
enum and ASCII messages are documented in the source javadoc.

Wired from the GetCharList response paths in
`server.gameserver.packets.client_tcp.GetCharList` (sub-action
3=delete, 5=check-name, 7=create-commit).

See `docs/protocol/flows/character_creation.md` and
`FINDINGS_2026-05-03_CHARDEL_SUBWAY.md` for end-to-end flow
documentation.
